overview: This image appears to show a Tortoise Bont Tick (Amblyomma marmoreum), a species of hard-backed tick. It is characterized by a reddish-brown body and a distinctive patterned white shield on its back. These ticks are commonly found on tortoises and can also parasitize other reptiles. They are categorized within the Ixodidae family of hard-bodied ticks. iNaturalist +2
